rag_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
generic_rules: Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Delete invalid clauses instead of adding architecture when references are unresolved
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `guarantee "label": expression;` for output range guarantees
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for helper declarations
Use `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for output assignments
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references absent from the analyzed model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
